scope: aggregate-only read-only audit of the 732 executions reconciled during the 15 July 08:10 BST startup

minorTelemetryInconsistency.detail: The failed run received two later system queue ingress/queued event pairs at 11:05:44Z and 11:53:18Z without a later execution transition or terminal result.
minorTelemetryInconsistency.classification: preserved queue telemetry inconsistency; not evidence that the task replayed or completed

decision.reason: The batch is internally consistent terminal history. Later successful doc-change and drift-repair work plus an empty pending-doc-change backlog remove the basis for replaying stale task identities.

toolInvocationLedger.requestedTask: Do the right next thing after the SQLite soak was durably scheduled.
toolInvocationLedger.evidence: OpenClaw tool results in the originating Telegram session
toolInvocationLedger.limitation: The generic audit found the task-run and SQLite surfaces but cannot inspect live protected task state or database aggregates.

toolInvocationLedger.requestedTask: Audit the 732 startup-reconciled executions without replay or payload access.
toolInvocationLedger.tool: core exec: sqlite3 -readonly with PRAGMA query_only and aggregate JSON-field queries; core source inspection with rg/sed
toolInvocationLedger.source: core OpenClaw tool and local project commands
toolInvocationLedger.fallbackReason: Direct aggregate inspection was required because coding-agent-skills is static audit-only and protected API access was not bypassed with credentials.

approvalBoundaries: replay or retry of historical executions
deletion, cleanup, or rewriting of execution/workflow evidence
service restart, migration, config or concurrency change
nextSafeStep: Preserve the batch. Keep the post-SQLite-window verification as the active integrity task; treat failed-idempotency-key re-enqueue telemetry hardening as a separate future code slice.
